title: pterospermadendron involucratum
intro: Pterospermadendron involucratum, a plant species native to Southeast Asia, is a member of the Malvaceae family and is commonly known as the 'sensitive plant' due to its sensitive leaves that fold up when touched. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Pterospermadendron involucratum include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or extracts, and are often used to treat respiratory issues and as a natural remedy for digestive problems.
